page.content: In December 2023, Philips Healthcare reports Managed Care enrollment with an amount of ₹1,200M and a year-over-year change of 15.2%, capturing a market share of 15.3%. Primary Care enrollment stands at ₹450M, reflecting a year-over-year change of 10.5%. Specialty Care follows with an enrollment value of ₹350M and a year-over-year change of 8.7%.

lines.text: In December 2023, Philips Healthcare reports Managed Care enrollment with an amount of ₹1,200M and a year-over-year
change of 15.2%, capturing a market share of 15.3%. Primary Care enrollment stands at ₹450M, reflecting a year-over-year
change of 10.5%. Specialty Care follows with an enrollment value of ₹350M and a year-over-year change of 8.7%.

page.content: Telehealth enrollment is recorded at ₹200M, showcasing a significant year-over-year change of 20.0%. Home Care has an enrollment amount of ₹100M, with a year-over-year change of 5.0%. Overall, the growth in managed care enrollment totals ₹2,400M, with a year-over-year change of 12.0%. In terms of outpatient visit trends, the volume for 2021 was ₹1,500M, with a year-over-year change of 5.0%. The volume increased to ₹1,800M in 2022, reflecting a year-over-year change of 20.0%, and further rose to ₹2,100M in 2023, demonstrating a year-over-year change of 16.7%. Philips holds a market share of 25.0% in the outpatient visit segment, while competitors account for 75.0%, culminating in a total market share of 100.0%.

page.content: This comprehensive analysis underscores Philips Healthcare's significant presence and growth trajectory within the Asia-Pacific healthcare sector.
